SUMMARY

This is AI generated summarization, which may have errors. For context, always refer to the full article.

SUPERSTAR. LeBron James of Team USA flexes.

USA Basketball Facebook page

PARIS, France – Four-time NBA champion LeBron James will be Team USA’s male flag bearer in the opening ceremony of the Paris Olympics on July 26, the American team said on Monday, July 22.

The two-time Olympic gold medalist, aged 39, will be participating in his fourth Olympics.

“It’s an incredible honor to represent the United States on this global stage, especially in a moment that can bring the whole world together,” James said in a statement.

“For a kid from Akron, this responsibility means everything to not only myself, but to my family, all the kids in my hometown, my teammates, fellow Olympians, and so many people across the country with big aspirations.”

“Sports have the power to bring us all together and I’m proud to be a part of this important moment.”

His female counterpart, who will also carry the flag on a barge along with boats carrying all other teams down the river Seine in front of 300,000 spectators, will be announced on Tuesday.

James and his flag bearer counterpart were chosen by a vote of fellow Team USA athletes.– Rappler.com

title: LeBron James picked as Team USA's flag bearer for Paris Games
author: delfin.dioquino editor
description: Participating in his fourth Olympics, NBA superstar LeBron James will serve as the United States' male flag bearer in the opening ceremony of the Paris Games
